data.title: Significant USDD Transfer Detected by Whale Alert
data.description: According to PANews, Whale Alert has reported a substantial transfer of 410,734,184 USDD from an address associated with Justin Sun to a wallet beginning with TXCu9. Blockchain data indicates that this TXCu9 address is labeled as the 'USDD MultiSig Signer' wallet.

data.title: Elon Musk: Meme tokens are a bit like casinos, it’s stupid to expect to win by gambling
data.description: Elon Musk said about the meme token phenomenon in an interview with Joe Rogan’s podcast: “It’s a bit like a casino. If you want to win by gambling, you’re stupid. Don’t invest your life savings in meme tokens. If you want to have some fun, you can play.”

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Criticizes Roger Ver's Life Sentence
data.description: According to Odaily, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has expressed his views on the sentencing of 'Bitcoin Jesus' Roger Ver, calling a life sentence for non-violent tax offenses absurd. Buterin suggested that the case against Ver appears to be politically motivated. He compared it to the situation of Ross Ulbricht, founder of the dark web marketplace Silk Road, noting that many individuals and companies accused of more severe crimes have received significantly lighter sentences than Ver. Buterin criticized the alleged actions of the IRS if they indeed threatened Ver's attorney to obtain confidential information, labeling it as malicious. He emphasized that the right to confidential legal consultations must be inviolably protected. Buterin argued that genuine errors should be rectified by allowing individuals to pay their taxes, including interest and penalties, rather than through prosecution.

data.title: Vitalik: Roger Ver's life sentence for tax crimes is ridiculous
data.description: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin wrote about "Bitcoin Jesus" Roger Ver, saying that it is absurd to be sentenced to life imprisonment for non-violent tax crimes. Vitalik said that the case against Roger Ver seems to be highly politically motivated; like Ross Ulbricht, the founder of the dark web Silk Road, many people and companies have been charged with more serious crimes but face much lighter sentences than Roger faces. If the IRS did threaten Roger's lawyers to obtain confidential information, it was an act of bad faith; the right of lawyers to consult confidentially must be protected inviolably. True good faith mistakes should be corrected by giving the parties the opportunity to pay taxes (including interest and penalties), not by prosecution.
